sutta:
7 9 now the venerable ananda went to visit the vulnerable sariputta and on coming to him greeted him courteously as he said at 1 site the venable ananda said this to the venable sariputta pray sariputta your reverence what is the reason what is the cause why certain beings in this world are not fully set free in this very life in this matter and under your reverence beings do not understand as it really is this perception partakes of worsening they do not understand as it really is this perception partakes of stability they do not understand as it really is this perception partakes of distinction they do not understand as it really is this perception partakes of penetration this your reverence is the reason this is the cause why some beings in this world are not fully set free in this very life and verbal analander again asked but pray sarikuta your reverence what is the reason what is the cause why certain beings in this world are fully set free in this very life in this matter your reverence beings do understand as it really is this perception partakes of worsening this perception partakes of distinction this perception partakes of stability and this but perception partakes of penetration this is the reason why certain beings are set free in this very life but that's the end of this now according to the diga nikaya sutra number 34 this suta refers to 4 kinds of concentration and this is also explained in the visudi maga in the visudi maga it says that [Music] of these forms of concentration the partaking of worsening or decline is due to the frequent arising of opposing states which means the hindrances probably [Music] then the partaking of stability is due to the persistence of that mindfulness which is in conformity with concentration the partaking of distinction is due to the attainment of a higher distinctive state which probably means vajana and the partaking of penetration is due to the promptings of perception and attention associated with disgust that means this is more of a like getting inside so you have discussed and dispassionate listener so uh so from here you can see that when we uh practice meditation we have to go through these 4 states the state of worsening which is the initial stage in our meditation our concentration is not stable so sometimes if you progress sometimes you'll regress sometimes you progress you take 1 step forward and then later you take 2 steps back sometimes you take 3 steps forward and then you take 1 step back like that now so this is uh the worsening stage then later you come to the second stage of stability where you can get into a tranquil state now every time you meditate every time you sit down you because of experience you are able to bring your mind down into a tranquil state so that you can use it then the third 1 is distinction that you that means you achieve a state of distinction and this i should refer to the jaanas the 1-pointedness of mind in the monk's precepts there is 1 precept in the paradigms the very heaviest offenses for among where if a monk does not have supernormal attainments and he claims to have supernormal attainments then he is barajika is no more among and in this definition of supernormal attainments 1 is arya stages that means he's not an arya he claims to be an arya second 1 he does not have psychic power he claims to have psychic power another 1 is he does not have jhana and he claims to have jhana that means he purposely willfully lies so so because of that definition of supernormal states so uh jana is a state of uh supernormal attainment a state of distinction that's why here i should refer to the janasa and then the the last 1 is uh the perception of this uh penetration now that means uh he gets inside now as a result of the uh 1 pointedness of mind so we have to go through these 4 states to get liberation in this very life so here you can see the importance of both samatha and vipassana in the liberation of a person
